meta.session: Session 3 — Nation Duel Day 1 complete, Firebase/GitHub deployment complete
meta.notes: Player name: Baba_Zulu. Screen values are authoritative — include VIP, technology, buildings, equipment, officers. Loadout-dependent; timestamp indicates active standard loadout.

city_stats.note: Troops Details shows idle+deployed troops. Gap of ~306,220 = troops in active fleets (286,812 deployed) + ~19,408 wounded in hospital (not shown in Troops Details).
city_stats.units_note: Total Units = sum of (troop_count × units_per_troop) for all troops. Shredders=10, Tanks=5, Bikes=5, Cannons=5, Shielders=5, EMP=10, Heavy Armored Soldier=2, all others=1. Troops Details shows troop counts (not unit counts). Difference between Total Units and Troops Details sum is combat losses from the day — no wounded in hospital at time of screenshot.

fleet.cap_note: 10,000 base + 174,462 bonus = 184,462 total. Includes Morgan fleet % bonus. Varies by Strategy officer appointed.
fleet.zombie_pool_note: Zombie pool units do NOT count against fleet cap. Verified: Fleet 2 = 184,462 non-zombie units + 17,600 zombie units = 202,062 total, but displays as 184,462/184,462 fleet cap used.

middle_special.buffed_by: Zombie-specific skills only — NOT buffed by mid-range skills
middle_special.role: Special damage dealer — buffed by Zephyrine and zombie-specific officer skills

troops.note: Counts from City Info > Troops Details. Does not include wounded in hospital (~19,408 estimated) or troops currently deployed in fleets (~286,812). Total Units per City Info = 860,074.

special_ability.name: Sniper Hit
special_ability.description: Every 3 attacks deals 1.8x damage to single enemy

special_ability.name: Overshoot
special_ability.mechanic: Every 3rd attack deals heavy damage to a single enemy, prioritizing the enemy back row within range.
special_ability.damage_characteristic: Single target, high damage — exact multiplier unconfirmed
special_ability.strategic_note: Back row sniping ability. Bypasses enemy front row to hit their highest-value units. In PvP this directly threatens enemy cannons and snipers.

special_ability.name: Bulletproof Armor
special_ability.mechanic: When taking damage, 31% chance to block 50% of that hit's damage.
special_ability.trigger: Each incoming hit — probabilistic, always active
special_ability.applies_to: All Tank tier units — same mechanic, stronger base stats at higher tiers
special_ability.strategic_note: Always-active probabilistic reduction. Averages 15.5% damage reduction per incoming hit.

special_ability.name: Shield Barrier
special_ability.mechanic: Every 4th attack triggers a shield on a friendly unit, absorbing a certain amount of incoming damage.
special_ability.visual: Explosion hitting a shield while troops are protected — incoming damage being blocked, not outgoing AoE
special_ability.combat_role: Defensive utility — reduces friendly troop losses, extends fight duration
special_ability.optimization_note: Immortal Shielder's value is NOT pure DPS — the shield absorption adds effective HP to friendly units fleet-wide. Raw eDPS/unit of 754 understates its contribution in a sustained fight.

special_ability.name: Bomb Launcher
special_ability.mechanic: Hits 3 different targets per activation. Damage per target and activation frequency unconfirmed — no reliable reference found. Best assumption based on other bomb-type skill descriptions in game.
special_ability.assumed_frequency: Unknown — likely every N attacks similar to Sniper Hit pattern
special_ability.effective_dps_multiplier_conservative: 1.5–2.0x (assuming reduced damage per target or partial activation)
special_ability.optimization_note: Even at conservative 1.5x multiplier, Death Cannon would be 739 * 1.5 = 1,109 eDPS/unit — significantly beating Eagle Eye Shot (816). At full 3x it would be 2,217 eDPS/unit, making it the best unit in the entire roster by a wide margin. The Bomb Launcher uncertainty is the single biggest variable in back row optimization.

T6_and_below.note: Filler and gathering only — no meaningful combat value

troops.difference_note: Calculated 860,074 units from Troops Details × units_per_troop = 860,074 City Info total. Exact match confirms all counts correct. No wounded in hospital at screenshot time. Any shortfall vs prior session counts = permanent combat losses.

zephyr_zero.name: Zephyr_Zero

system_mechanics.equipment_skills: Level via equipment upgrade, separate from skill XP
system_mechanics.skill_reset_cost: 10% of accumulated skill XP lost on reset — skill drops to Lv.1
system_mechanics.unequipped_skills: Remain at Lv.1 — can only gain XP while equipped on officer
system_mechanics.star_locked_slots: Skills cannot be equipped until officer reaches required star level

equipment.unlock_condition: 3★ (Star 3) — equipment slot on LEFT side of officer screen becomes available
equipment.description: Equipment is a distinct upgrade path from skills. Skills are on the RIGHT side, equipment on the LEFT.
equipment.equipment_provides: Its own set of stat effects — separate from and additive to skill bonuses
equipment.upgrade_resources: Different resource type from skill leveling

storm_bow_equipment.priority: HIGH — All Troops DEF/HP base skill makes equipment effects potentially very valuable

implications_for_optimization: 3★ unlocks BOTH a new skill slot AND equipment — double upgrade at the same threshold
Equipment effects are separate from skill pool — purely additive
Officers without equipment (pre-3★) are missing a full upgrade layer
When evaluating officers, note whether equipment is active or locked

nation_duel_recommendation.slot_1: Doc Gray — Elite Pacemaker +11 gives +7% vs all enemy rows (highest)
nation_duel_recommendation.slot_2: I. Medici — Gold Flames +10 gives +6.4% vs all enemy rows
nation_duel_recommendation.not_used: F. Medici — identical stats to I.Medici but lower mastery, no advantage
nation_duel_recommendation.combined_contribution: +13.4% Attack Against all enemy rows from Director slots
nation_duel_recommendation.note: All three equipment items provide Attack Against Row Boost to all three rows. Doc Gray is strictly better due to higher equipment level. Directors ARE worth fielding for Nation Duel — +13.4% vs all rows is meaningful.

monster_combat.titan_plane_deployment: Both titans + plane always used for monsters — no vitality cost

elite_mine_mechanics.load_limit_reached: Rarely — fleet load capacity ~2,959,012 for combat fleet
elite_mine_mechanics.gold_mine_advantage: Solo, predictable duration — ideal for overnight pre-event pre-load

strategy.pre_event_overnight: 1-2 fleets on gold Lv4-5 mines (guaranteed points, predictable), 1 fleet on grain/oil timed to finish after event start
strategy.event_start: Withdraw all pre-loaded fleets immediately for instant points
strategy.during_event: 3-4 fleets on grain/oil elite mines, 1 fleet always free for combat
strategy.note: Gathering is secondary contributor to Strongest Commander score. Single KE fight worth 1M+ pts. Optimize for passive efficiency, not maximum micromanagement.

cumulative_army_stats.note: Authoritative totals. Include all active sources: officers, equipment, equipment expertise, technology, buildings, VIP. Loadout-dependent — reflects standard combat loadout.

increases_damage_to.note: Shown as separate category in Details screen. Likely includes Zeus Precise Shot (7.8%) + VIP and/or equipment expertise. Mechanic vs own_troop_attack unclear — treating as separate for now.

attack_against_enemy_row.note: Separate category in Details screen. Mechanic relationship to own_troop_attack buffs unclear — treating as separate multiplier until confirmed.

enemy_debuffs_applied.note: Reductions applied to enemy troops when fighting Baba_Zulu.

cross_nation_battle.note: All Cross-Nation Battle bonuses currently +0% — not invested.

development_stats.note: Max wounded total includes buildings, technology, Bravestar, officer IMT skills, equipment Medic Expert skills, and VIP.

fleet_2_monster_attack.name: Monster Attack
fleet_2_monster_attack.zombie_note: T10 zombies fully included (2,430/2,430), T9 partially (15,170/35,859). Lower tiers excluded. Only highest available tiers used.

middle_zombie_pool.note: Separate pool — does not consume fleet cap

notable: Blaster Rocket Launcher T8 and Sharpshooter Sniper T8 excluded — not used for monsters
T8/T7/T6/T5 zombies excluded — only T10+T9 used
Fleet cap exactly met at 184,462 non-zombie units
Tanks (25,000 units) and Shredders (38,800 units) dominate front row — heavy damage sponge

group_match.format: Round robin — each Nation duels every other Nation in group

finals.seeding: Ranked 1-12 by Group Match Nation Points (highest = Group 1)
finals.progression: Win = advance 1 Duel Group per day; Lose = drop 1 Duel Group per day
finals.nation_points_bonus: Higher Duel Group = more Nation Points bonus (does NOT affect Individual Points)
finals.final_ranking_basis: Nation Points after all 4 Finals competitions

points.individual_points: Earned by confronting enemy Nation Commanders
points.nation_points_contributors: Top 200 Commanders' Individual Points each day
points.top_200_selection_day2plus: By Individual Points from previous day
points.daily_winner: Nation with more Nation Points wins the day

normal_enemy.troop_type: System Troops — NOT real troops, zero loss risk
normal_enemy.battle_end_condition: All 24 enemies defeated OR Commander defeated
normal_enemy.fortify_duel: Duel Tokens or fortification times → Fortify Duel (same day only; select some to inherit next day)

elite_enemy.format: Auto-battle vs 1 matched enemy Commander at same Individual Points rank

fortification_collection.acquisition: Free times + Duel Tokens → 1 random from 3 options
fortification_collection.inheritance: Selected fortifications carry forward each day; count increases over event

optimization_context.note: For Nation Duel, real troops are NOT used — System Troops replace them at T13 equivalent strength. The optimization variables are entirely officer, equipment, titan, warplane, and technology bonuses inherited by System Troops.
optimization_context.key_insight: With all troops standardized at T13, the relative value of row-specific vs universal buffs changes vs normal combat. Cross-Nation battle stats (currently all +0% for Baba_Zulu) become relevant investment targets for this event.
optimization_context.cross_nation_stats_current: All +0% — no Cross-Nation Battle bonuses invested yet
optimization_context.formation_optimization: Pending — Baba_Zulu to ask formation optimization question

special_ability.name: Electromagnetic Conduction
special_ability.mechanic: Every 3rd attack casts chain lightning dealing heavy damage to up to 3 units, prioritizing enemy back row within range. Damage can stack on a single target.
special_ability.targets: Up to 3 (can stack all on 1 target)
special_ability.damage_characteristic: AoE chain up to 3 units OR concentrated on 1 target — exact multiplier unconfirmed
special_ability.strategic_note: Superior to Overshoot in flexibility — same back-row priority but can either splash 3 targets or concentrate all damage on one. If stacked on single target, potentially 3x the damage of a normal Overshoot hit. This makes Havoc Breaker considerably more dangerous than raw stats suggest.

special_ability.combat_role: Defensive utility — reduces friendly troop losses, extends fight duration
special_ability.optimization_note: Immortal Shielder's value is NOT pure DPS — the shield absorption adds effective HP to friendly units fleet-wide. Raw eDPS/unit of 754 understates its contribution in a sustained fight.

dps_per_unit_effective_ranking.note: Best by far — 1.0s cooldown is the key

dps_per_unit_effective_ranking.name: Eagle Eye Shot

dps_per_unit_effective_ranking.name: Destruction Tank
dps_per_unit_effective_ranking.note: Front — same DPS as Infantry but 5x HP/unit, better tank

dps_per_unit_effective_ranking.name: Hell King
dps_per_unit_effective_ranking.note: Front — lowest DPS but Defensive Stance -34% dmg reduction

key_findings: Demon Raider (Bike T12) remains #1 — 1.0s cooldown makes it 30% better than Hades Gatling and 70% better than everything else in middle
SURPRISE: Immortal Shielder (T13) is the best back row unit (754 eDPS/unit) beating Death Cannon (739) — 326.4 vs 320 atk/unit
Immortal Shielder also has range 3.8 equal to Eagle Eye Shot — no range disadvantage vs Death Cannon's 4.0
Middle row dominates — top 3 spots all middle row units. Middle buff (+267.6%) vs Long-range (+202%) is a significant gap
Front row units are pure damage sponges — Apocalypse Infantry has highest HP/unit (2643) of all front units
Hell King has Defensive Stance ability — actual value depends on fight duration, likely better than raw DPS suggests
Zombie pool (50,000 units) always use — 7.01x effective stack makes them extremely efficient free DPS
Formation priority: maximize Demon Raiders in middle, Immortal Shielders in back, minimum viable front row
pending: Special abilities for T12/T13 units not yet captured (need to tap ability icons)
Hell King Defensive Stance details — same mechanic as Wasteland King?
Formation constraints — minimum units per row to activate row buffs unclear
Whether Freya Mid+Long buff applies in Nation Duel (Drillmaster active?)

complete_effective_dps_ranking.name: Demon Raider
complete_effective_dps_ranking.note: 1.0s CD is decisive — fires 2x as fast as everything else

complete_effective_dps_ranking.name: Immortal Shielder
complete_effective_dps_ranking.note: SURPRISE: Best back row unit, 326 atk/unit

complete_effective_dps_ranking.name: Fanatic Terminator
complete_effective_dps_ranking.note: Middle filler if Demon Raiders exhausted

complete_effective_dps_ranking.name: Destruction Tank
complete_effective_dps_ranking.note: Front — same as Infantry but 5 units/troop, needs more cap

complete_effective_dps_ranking.name: Hell King
complete_effective_dps_ranking.note: Front — Defensive Stance ability may compensate

optimization_analysis.special_ability_note: Confirmed: T12/T13 units use identical ability mechanics to lower-tier equivalents. Higher tier = stronger base stats only. Row buffs always active regardless of troop count in that row — tokens (1 unit) are valid.

speed_racer_impact.note: Demon Raider fires 60% more attacks in the opening 10 seconds. Combined with its already-dominant 1.0s base CD, the opening burst is extremely high. In PvP where fights can be decided early, this further separates Demon Raider from all other middle units.

sniper_hit_impact.unit: Eagle Eye Shot / Immortal Shielder context
sniper_hit_impact.note: Eagle Eye Shot gets Sniper Hit (every 3rd attack 1.8x damage = +26.7% average DPS uplift). Effective eDPS/unit becomes ~644.5 * 1.267 = 816.4 — which would put it ABOVE Death Cannon (739) and close to Immortal Shielder (754). Sniper Hit makes Eagle Eye Shot more competitive than raw stats suggest. Immortal Shielder ability unknown — if purely defensive, Eagle Eye Shot may rival or beat it for pure damage.

revised_back_row_assessment.note: Immortal Shielder is NOT a pure damage unit — its shield ability provides defensive utility that extends friendly troop survival. This changes the calculus significantly.

back_row_ranking_revised.name: Eagle Eye Shot
back_row_ranking_revised.ability: Sniper Hit — every 3rd attack 1.8x damage (+26.7% avg DPS)
back_row_ranking_revised.role: Pure single-target DPS — best back row damage dealer once ability factored in

back_row_ranking_revised.name: Death Cannon
back_row_ranking_revised.ability_adjusted_dps: Unknown — Bomb Launcher AoE multiplier TBD
back_row_ranking_revised.ability: Bomb Launcher — hits multiple targets, exact mechanic unconfirmed
back_row_ranking_revised.role: Likely best back row if Bomb Launcher hits 2+ targets; each additional target adds full 739 eDPS/unit equivalent

back_row_ranking_revised.name: Immortal Shielder
back_row_ranking_revised.ability: Shield Barrier — every 4th attack shields a friendly unit, absorbs incoming damage
back_row_ranking_revised.role: Defensive support — extends friendly troop survival. Best used in combination with high-DPS units, not as primary damage dealer.

back_row_ranking_revised.name: Ultima EMP Cannon
back_row_ranking_revised.ability: Unknown — likely debuff or AoE pulse
back_row_ranking_revised.role: Back row filler pending ability confirmation

revised_back_row_assessment.recommendation: Death Cannon + Eagle Eye Shot likely optimal back row mix. Immortal Shielder valuable as a support component — a modest allocation shields your Demon Raiders and extends the fight, letting your superior middle row DPS accumulate. Pure damage maximization favors Cannon + Sniper; survivability-weighted favors adding some Shielders.

bomb_launcher_scenarios.conclusion: Death Cannon wins unless Bomb Launcher deals ≤33% damage per target (i.e. total damage = 1.0x single target). Given the skill is designed as an offensive ability, the 33% or less scenario seems unlikely. Best estimate: Death Cannon is the superior back row damage dealer.
bomb_launcher_scenarios.recommended_approach: Use Death Cannon as primary back row. Eagle Eye Shot as secondary fill. Small Immortal Shielder allocation for survivability support on Demon Raiders.

back_row_sniping_mechanics.mechanic: Every 3rd attack prioritizes enemy back row — bypasses their front row to directly damage their cannons/snipers
back_row_sniping_mechanics.pvp_significance: In PvP, enemy back row is their Death Cannons and Eagle Eye Shots — their highest DPS units. Overshoot and EC attacks go directly to them every 3rd hit, reducing enemy back row output faster. This is a force multiplier beyond raw DPS: killing enemy cannons early reduces the damage your front row absorbs.

Havoc Breaker.proc_ability: EC — up to 3 targets OR stacked on 1, back row priority

havoc_breaker_vs_fanatic_terminator.verdict: Havoc Breaker is strictly better — higher base DPS AND superior proc (EC can hit 3 or stack vs Overshoot's 1). No reason to use Fanatic Terminator if Havoc Breakers are available.

ranking.name: Demon Raider
ranking.proc: Speed Racer +60% atk speed first 10s — pure DPS, no back-row targeting

ranking.name: Hades Gatling
ranking.proc: Rapid Fire — DPS boost, no back-row targeting (unconfirmed exact mechanic)

ranking.name: Havoc Breaker
ranking.proc: EC every 3 attacks — up to 3 targets or stacked, back row priority. Strictly better than Fanatic Terminator.

ranking.name: Fanatic Terminator
ranking.proc: Overshoot every 3 attacks — 1 target, back row priority. Inferior to Havoc Breaker in every dimension.

revised_middle_row_assessment.note: Demon Raider + Hades Gatling for raw DPS. Havoc Breaker adds back-row disruption. Fanatic Terminator is obsolete if Havoc Breakers are available.

metric_definitions.sustained_base_dps_per_unit: Base attack / cooldown × buff stack, divided by units_per_troop. Normal attacks only, no special abilities, assumes infinite fight duration. Valid for apples-to-apples comparison of normal attack output per fleet cap unit.
metric_definitions.opening_burst_dps_per_unit: sustained_base_dps_per_unit adjusted for Speed Racer (+60% atk speed) during first 10s of combat. Only applies to Demon Raider (Bike type). Demon Raider opening CD = 0.625s → 1.6x normal attack rate for 10s.
metric_definitions.proc_dps_contribution: Additional damage from special abilities (Sniper Hit, Overshoot, EC, Bomb Launcher). Adds to sustained_base_dps but on separate timelines. Most procs trigger every 3rd or 4th attack.
metric_definitions.attrition_adjusted_dps: AoE/multi-target abilities (Bomb Launcher, EC) lose value as enemy units thin. Single-target abilities (Sniper Hit, Overshoot) remain constant throughout. Speed Racer irrelevant after 10s. Shield Barrier most valuable early.

notes: Speed Racer active — Demon Raider fires at 0.625s CD (1.6x rate)
Enemy formation full — AoE abilities (Bomb Launcher, EC) hit maximum targets
Shield Barrier absorbing maximum incoming fire
Dominant unit: Demon Raider by large margin
Speed Racer expired — Demon Raider back to 1.0s CD
Enemy formation still mostly intact — AoE still near full value
Sniper Hit, Overshoot, EC all contributing at full rate
Dominant unit: Demon Raider still leads, gap narrows vs Hades Gatling
Enemy front row likely eliminated — all units dynamically shift to middle/back row targets
Bomb Launcher still finds 3 targets (middle + back row) via target shifting
EC chain still finds 3 targets or stacks on surviving high-HP units
When all troops eliminated, units attack enemy titans (~20M HP) and plane (~24M HP)
Single-target procs (Sniper Hit, Overshoot) fully effective against titans/plane
Shield Barrier less useful — fewer incoming attacks as enemy thins
Speed Racer long expired — Demon Raider at base 1.0s CD, still competitive

Demon Raider.reason: Single-target normal attacks + Speed Racer only matters in opening. Consistent throughout.

Hades Gatling.reason: Single-target, consistent. Rapid Fire proc details unconfirmed but likely single-target.

Havoc Breaker.reason: EC hits up to 3 but can stack on 1 — late fight stacking on surviving targets maintains value.

Death Cannon.reason: Bomb Launcher hitting 3 targets loses 67% of multi-target value if only 1 enemy remains.

Eagle Eye Shot.reason: Sniper Hit is single-target — full value throughout fight.

Immortal Shielder.reason: Shield Barrier most useful early when incoming fire is heaviest.

Hell King.reason: Defensive Stance is % reduction — scales with incoming damage naturally.

Fanatic Terminator.reason: Overshoot single-target — consistent. But inferior to Havoc Breaker throughout.

damage_framework.long_fight_ranking_shift: In long fights: Death Cannon loses relative value vs Eagle Eye Shot as Bomb Launcher targets thin. Eagle Eye Shot's Sniper Hit maintains full value. Demon Raider's Speed Racer advantage disappears but base 1.0s CD still wins. Havoc Breaker EC stacking on single targets may actually increase in value late — concentrating full chain damage on one remaining high-HP target.

target_acquisition.mechanic: Units dynamically retarget as enemy formation units are eliminated. When a unit type is fully destroyed, attacking units shift to next available targets within range. Order generally: front row → middle → back → titans/plane.

implications_for_aoe_abilities.Bomb_Launcher: 3 targets remain available throughout most of fight via target shifting. As enemy front depletes, Bomb Launcher hits middle+back instead. Only truly degrades when total surviving enemy units < 3 — near end of fight only. Death Cannon attrition sensitivity REVISED to LOW-MEDIUM.
implications_for_aoe_abilities.EC_chain: 3-target chain shifts to next available enemy units as rows eliminated. Stacking on single target remains option when fewer than 3 enemies survive. Havoc Breaker value shape changes late but doesn't collapse.
implications_for_aoe_abilities.Overshoot: Single target, back-row priority. Once enemy back row eliminated, shifts to next row. Consistent.

titan_and_plane_as_late_targets.note: When all enemy troops eliminated, units attack enemy titans and plane. Titans ~19-22M HP, plane ~24M HP at typical levels — significant final DPS sink. All abilities remain effective. Bomb Launcher hitting titan+plane = still 2 targets.

revised_attrition_sensitivity.Demon Raider: LOW — single target throughout; Speed Racer irrelevant after 10s but base 1.0s CD still leads
revised_attrition_sensitivity.Havoc Breaker: LOW-MEDIUM — EC shifts targets dynamically; stacking vs spreading is tactical not forced by attrition
revised_attrition_sensitivity.Death Cannon: LOW-MEDIUM — Bomb Launcher finds 3 targets throughout via target shift; only degrades at very end
revised_attrition_sensitivity.Eagle Eye Shot: LOW — Sniper Hit single target, consistent including vs titans
revised_attrition_sensitivity.Immortal Shielder: MEDIUM — Shield Barrier value tied to volume of incoming fire, decreases as enemy thins
revised_attrition_sensitivity.Hell King: LOW — Defensive Stance % reduction scales naturally with incoming damage level
revised_attrition_sensitivity.Fanatic Terminator: LOW — Overshoot shifts targets as rows eliminated, consistent

target_acquisition.key_revision: Previous analysis overstated attrition penalty for AoE units. Dynamic target acquisition means Bomb Launcher and EC chain maintain near-full value for almost the entire fight. Death Cannon vs Eagle Eye Shot is therefore a sustained base DPS + proc frequency question, not an attrition question. Death Cannon's Bomb Launcher advantage persists throughout the fight.

front_row_comparison.verdict: Destruction Tank wins on all metrics — higher HP, Def, and marginally better avg reduction. Hell King niche: high-burst enemy where 34% spike absorption matters more than average.

scoring_and_strategy.points_source: Killing enemy troops — points scale with units destroyed

normal_enemy.opponent: Random enemy commanders — unknown formation and skills each day
normal_enemy.attempts: Unlimited replays per day (costs gold per retry)
normal_enemy.formation_flexibility: Can change formation between each retry
normal_enemy.buff_flexibility: Additional buffs (fortifications) can be applied between retries if available/earned
normal_enemy.score_tracking: Cumulative points — highest single run recorded, retries can improve score
normal_enemy.strategy_implication: Since opponent is unknown and formation can change between runs, the optimal approach is: (1) run a first attempt to scout the enemy composition, (2) adjust formation to counter what you see, (3) retry with optimized setup. Gold cost of retries must be weighed against incremental points gained.
unknown_variables: Enemy buff levels (their officers, equipment, fortifications)

elite_enemy.opponent: Specific matched commander from enemy nation, paired by Individual Points rank
elite_enemy.attempts: One auto-battle per day at 23:00 server time — formation locks at that point
elite_enemy.formation_flexibility: Must be set before 23:00 — no changes after lock
elite_enemy.intelligence_available: After first duel, can examine enemy's previous battle history for formation hints
elite_enemy.intelligence_limitations: Past formation is indicative only — enemy can change daily
elite_enemy.score_implication: Win = +30% Individual Points bonus; Loss = 0% bonus; Bye = +10%
elite_enemy.strategy_implication: Elite Enemy is the high-stakes fixed commitment. Formation must be finalized before 23:00 based on best available intelligence. On Day 1 no prior battle data exists — must use best general-purpose formation. Day 2+ can incorporate observed enemy behavior.

fortification_buffs.note: Fortification buffs change daily as new ones are earned and fused. Stack grows over event.
fortification_buffs.implication: Buff stack is not static — the +160% figure is current but will shift. Formation optimization must be recalculated if buff profile changes significantly, particularly if row-specific fortifications are earned that favor certain unit types.

optimization_layers.layer_1_general_formation: Best formation against unknown opponent — maximize sustained DPS across all rows, robust to any enemy composition. This is the Day 1 Elite Enemy formation and the starting point for Normal Enemy first attempts.
optimization_layers.layer_2_counter_formation: Adjusted formation after scouting enemy in Normal Enemy replays. If enemy is front-heavy: maximize back row and back-row-sniping units (Havoc Breaker, Overshoot). If enemy is back-row-heavy: maximize front row durability to survive long enough for your back row to fire. If enemy has strong zombies: Zephyrine's queen bonus gives your zombies a relative edge.
optimization_layers.layer_3_elite_enemy_specific: Day 2+ formation tuned to observed Elite Enemy behavior. Must balance counter-strategy against uncertainty that they may change formation.

gold_retry_economics.note: Cost per retry unknown — need to track gold cost vs incremental points to determine break-even.
gold_retry_economics.general_principle: Early retries after scouting adjustment likely highest value. Diminishing returns on later retries once formation is already well-matched.
gold_retry_economics.pending: Record gold cost per retry and points gained per retry to build break-even model

recommended_formation_day1.description: General-purpose Day 1 formation — robust against unknown enemy composition
recommended_formation_day1.note: DPS estimate excludes proc abilities (Bomb Launcher, Sniper Hit, EC, Speed Racer burst) and zombie contribution

troops.name: Destruction Tank
troops.role: Primary front — Bulletproof Armor 15.5% avg reduction, highest HP+Def/unit
troops.note: Replaces Hell King as primary front. T13 higher stats across the board.

front_row.note: Destruction Tank T13 preferred over Hell King T12: higher HP/unit (2,643 vs 2,226), higher Def/unit (160 vs 138), and Bulletproof Armor (15.5% avg) slightly edges Defensive Stance (14.45% avg). Hell King retains niche vs high-burst enemies.

troops.name: Death Cannon

troops.name: Immortal Shielder

troops.name: Biochemical Zombie
troops.role: Free DPS — 7.01x effective stack + Zephyrine bonuses

recommended_officers.warfare_swap: Alexandria (Dmg Taken -6.8%) can replace Storm Bow for more offensive lean

strategy_2_slots.name: Loreline
strategy_2_slots.key_skill: Base skills irrelevant — equipped for vs Enemy Rows +7.6% combat buff from Double Gun equipment

strategy_2_slots.name: Morgan or Bravestar

recommended_officers.strategy_note: Morgan and Saki fleet buffs are worthless at fixed 400K system troops. Loreline's equipment is the only Strategy officer combat contribution.

drillmaster_6_slots.name: Freya
drillmaster_6_slots.key_skill: Armor Assault: Mid+Long Atk +12.96% — buffs both dominant DPS rows

drillmaster_6_slots.name: Maria
drillmaster_6_slots.key_skill: Eagle Eye: All Atk +8,400,000 flat at 400K units (4.3x normal army value)

drillmaster_6_slots.name: Cattie
drillmaster_6_slots.key_skill: Sharp Senses: Enemy Atk -12.96% — highest enemy attack debuff

drillmaster_6_slots.name: Layla
drillmaster_6_slots.key_skill: Morale Boost: First 20s All Atk +9.345% + Mid/Long Weaken

drillmaster_6_slots.name: Simon
drillmaster_6_slots.key_skill: Open Fire: First 20s All Atk +13.7% — replaces Selina since IMT is irrelevant in Nation Duel

recommended_officers.drillmaster_note: Simon + Layla opening burst (+23% combined first 20s) syncs directly with Demon Raider Speed Racer window. Selina's IMT is completely wasted here.

machinist_2_slots.name: Emily
machinist_2_slots.key_skill: Machine Awakening: All ATK/DEF/HP +20.99% effective at mastery 324

machinist_2_slots.name: Kay
machinist_2_slots.key_skill: Heavy Mecha: +3,672,000 flat attack / +20,808,000 flat HP at 400K units

director_2_slots.current_recommendation: I. Medici + Doc Gray — default pick but neither contributes to Nation Duel combat
director_2_slots.note: Both have only Medic Expert equipment (wounded capacity) — irrelevant in Nation Duel. Director slots are essentially wasted in this event unless other Directors have combat equipment.
director_2_slots.action_needed: Check equipment skills on Ginger, Jesse, Sergeant Barney, Steven, Victoria — if any have combat-relevant equipment (attack, defense, damage buffs), they should replace I.Medici/Doc Gray for Nation Duel.

titans_and_plane.note: Edgar + Zephyrine + Zephyr_Zero — no vitality cost in Nation Duel system troops context. Zephyrine queen bonus applies to T13 zombies.

design_rationale.middle_heavy_67pct: Middle row has strongest DPS/unit (Demon Raider 1292, Gatling 995) and highest row buff (+267.6%). Maximizing middle is the single biggest DPS lever.
design_rationale.front_18pct_minimum: Enough Hell Kings for Defensive Stance durability. Apocalypse Infantry as HP filler. Front absorbs damage while middle fires.
design_rationale.back_15pct: Death Cannon for Bomb Launcher multi-target. Eagle Eye for consistent Sniper Hit. Small Shielder allocation extends Demon Raider survival.
adjustment_triggers: If enemy is front-heavy: shift some Hades Gatling → more Havoc Breaker (more EC back-row sniping)
If enemy has very strong back row: shift some Demon Raider → more Death Cannon
If fight ends quickly (enemy weak): opening burst composition, add Simon for Open Fire
If fight is very long (enemy strong): sustained DPS composition, keep Eagle Eye Shot / reduce Shielder

simon_vs_selina.breakeven_true: Longer than 42.3s — Selina DEF/HP value not counted in attack-only calc
simon_vs_selina.day1_recommendation: Selina — safer against unknown enemy, permanent triple buff robust to any fight duration
simon_vs_selina.simon_swap_trigger: Swap Simon in after scouting reveals weak enemy or short fights (under ~35-40s)
simon_vs_selina.note: Layla already provides +9.345% opening 20s — Simon adds +13.7% on top for +23% combined burst, but only pays off in short fights

screen_1_summary.note: Top level — no unit detail here

screen_2_troops_detail.special_rows: First 3 rows are typically Titan1, Titan2, Warplane — show level not tier
screen_2_troops_detail.unit_identification: Icon only — use recognition_guide to map to unit name
screen_2_troops_detail.note: Scrollable — may need multiple screenshots to capture all units

screen_3_technology_bonus.note: MOST IMPORTANT SCREEN — actual buffs applied, ground truth for post-mortem

icon_recognition.method: Match tier badge (Roman numeral) + icon visual characteristics to identify unit

row_inference.front_indicators: High lost units early, lower enemy kills per unit, appears in first rows of list
row_inference.middle_indicators: Moderate survival, high enemy kills, bikes/gunners/storm troopers
row_inference.back_indicators: Higher survival, very high enemy kills per unit, cannons/snipers
row_inference.zombie_indicators: Distinct zombie icon, always grouped together, green/grey coloring
row_inference.titan_plane_indicators: Always surviving=1, large detailed icon, level number shown not tier

disambiguation_rules: Same unit type across tiers: distinguish by Roman numeral badge only
Bike types (Demon Raider/Storm Raider): horizontal low profile, tier badge is key
Cannon types (Death Cannon/Uranium Cannon): wheeled cannon silhouette, tier badge key
Shielder types: large shield prominent, tier badge key
Zombie: always distinctly green/grey hunched figure — unmistakable
Titans/Plane: shown at top of list, surviving=1, NO tier badge — show level number instead

sample_postmortem_uranium_mine.battle: Uranium Mine defense — Baba_Zulu vs Mr.Condictor
sample_postmortem_uranium_mine.kill_ratio: 76,188 friendly kills vs 214,562 enemy losses — 1:2.8 unfavorable
sample_postmortem_uranium_mine.dominant_enemy_unit: T9 Cannon — 106,841 kills = 49.7% of all enemy damage
recommendations: CRITICAL: Long-Range Attack gap of 188% is the primary loss driver. Upgrade Long-Range Attack technology research as highest priority.

HIGH: Enemy attack reduction debuffs are significantly weaker than opponent's. Review Drillmaster officers — Jimmy and Cattie provide enemy attack reduction. Ensure both are equipped and at maximum level.
HIGH: Opponent's T9 Cannon stack was overwhelming — in future mine defense, consider heavier front row to absorb cannon fire longer.
NOTE: Kingsley and Panda Jack base city skills do not apply in Uranium Mine battles — their equipment skills still apply but base skill buff is lost vs city defense.

hypothesis_framework.description: Formation decisions are hypotheses about expected outcomes. Post-mortem validates or refutes them.

pre_battle_inputs.formation: What units were deployed in each row and in what quantity
pre_battle_inputs.officer_loadout: Which officers were active and what buffs they contribute
pre_battle_inputs.expected_buffs: Predicted Technology Bonus values based on profile
pre_battle_inputs.formation_rationale: Why this formation — what is it optimized to do

post_battle_analysis.buff_validation: Compare expected vs actual Technology Bonus — discrepancies reveal officer/equip issues
post_battle_analysis.kill_efficiency: Kills per unit by row — did each row perform its expected role
post_battle_analysis.loss_pattern: Which of your units died first and fastest — reveals enemy formation strength
post_battle_analysis.enemy_unit_inference: Infer enemy formation from which of your units took most damage + enemy kill distribution
post_battle_analysis.hypothesis_verdict: CONFIRMED / PARTIALLY CONFIRMED / REFUTED with specific evidence

formation_revision_signals.enemy_back_row_heavy: Your front row died fast, enemy long-range killed most — increase Havoc Breaker or Storm Trooper
formation_revision_signals.enemy_front_row_heavy: Your back row underperformed — enemy melee absorbed damage — increase Bomb Launcher or EMP
formation_revision_signals.enemy_buff_dominant: Technology gap explains loss regardless of formation — research/officer upgrade priority
formation_revision_signals.formation_matched_hypothesis: Formation was correct — loss due to stat gap, not formation error

uranium_mine.hypothesis_focus: Survival and kill ratio — defend long enough to win points
uranium_mine.officer_note: Kingsley/Panda Jack base skills do NOT apply — adjust expected buffs accordingly
uranium_mine.troop_note: Real troops used — consider troop tier gaps vs opponent

nation_duel_normal.hypothesis_focus: Maximize kills against unknown system enemy — retry with adjustments
nation_duel_normal.officer_note: All officers apply — Technology Bonus should match profile exactly
nation_duel_normal.troop_note: System troops — no loss risk, unlimited retry value

nation_duel_elite.hypothesis_focus: Win the matchup — can scout opponent history from Day 2+
nation_duel_elite.officer_note: Formation locks at 23:00 — pre-battle hypothesis must be set before lock
nation_duel_elite.troop_note: System troops — formation locked so post-mortem informs NEXT day

buffs_active: second fortify buff earned from run 1
runs.notes: Score lower despite same waves — RNG variance confirmed

runs.formation_change: Tanks +10,000 troops (14K→24K), Hades Gatling -40,000 units (50K→10K troops), Simon replacing Selina
buffs_active: two fortify buffs from prior runs
runs.notes: Fewer kills despite same waves. Hades Gatling removal hurt more than extra tanks helped.

runs.formation_change: Demon Raider 40K→36K troops (-20K units), Hell King Shredder added 2K troops (+20K units front), Shielder retained, Selina
runs.verdict: SIGNIFICANTLY WORSE — -499 pts vs best run
runs.notes: Worst result of all four runs. Bike reduction confirmed as damaging.

runs.formation_change: Original formation restored — 40K bike troops, Shielder, Selina

observations.wave_mechanic: 6 waves completed both runs — enemy keeps sending armies until fleet exhausted
observations.no_troop_loss: System troops confirmed — 0 lost, 0 wounded, power loss 0
observations.rng_confirmed: Same formation + extra buff = lower score on run 2. RNG affects kill counts per wave.
observations.score_variance: 5991 vs 5920 = 71 point swing = 1.2% variance between identical runs
observations.fortify_mechanic: Run 1 earned Fortification +1 and Slot +3 as battle reward — stackable buffs

bonus_mechanic.confirmed: +12% bonus applied to base kill score

run_3.note: 5153 * 1.12 = 5771 — close but 26 off, may be rounding or slight variance

bonus_mechanic.implication: Every kill is worth 1.12 pts, not 1.0. Threshold to hit 6,000 is 6000/1.12 = 5,357 base kills score

Orange.equipment: Active from 3★ — left side slot with own stats + expertise

Purple.equipment: NO equipment system — skills only regardless of star level

base_skill_context.City Guards: Only applies defending your own city — NOT in Nation Duel or field
base_skill_context.Siege Army: Only applies attacking city gates — NOT in Nation Duel or field

equipment_resource.strategy: Hold for event bonuses — never spend outside events

nation_duel_mechanics.fleet: 400,000 system troops + 50,000 zombie pool — no permanent losses
nation_duel_mechanics.wave_mechanic: Enemies arrive sequentially and additively — 1, then 2, then 3 etc. simultaneously. Max 24 armies. Your single army fights continuously through escalating pressure.
nation_duel_mechanics.survivors: 450,003 always returned at end regardless of battle — not a performance metric
nation_duel_mechanics.fortify_buff: Earned as battle reward — +HP to all troops for next run
nation_duel_mechanics.elite_enemy: Formation locks at 23:00 — set before then

validated_formation.officer: Selina (sustained DEF/HP — confirmed better than Simon for attrition)

key_learnings: Demon Raider Speed Racer is the scoring engine — never reduce bike allocation
Early kills reduce incoming damage before next enemy army layers on
Hades Gatling pulls real weight at 50K troops — do not reduce
Front row bulk (shredder) does not compensate for lost mid-row firepower
Selina confirmed over Simon for wave attrition
Score variance ~100-200 pts per run from RNG alone
Path to wave 7 is next improvement target

Tank.name: Bulletproof Armor
Tank.description: 31% chance to block 50% of damage per hit
Tank.uptime: Always active — pure passive per hit

Infantry.name: Home Guard
Infantry.description: Damage reduction triggered every 8 seconds — damage taken reduced 50% for 4 seconds
Infantry.note: Same cycle as Shredder Defensive Stance but higher reduction (50% vs 34%) — weaker unit stats though

Armored Soldier.name: Reflective Armor
Armored Soldier.description: 35% chance to reflect 20% of damage taken back to attacker
Armored Soldier.note: Weakest passive of all front-row types — primarily offensive via reflection rather than pure defense

Bike.name: Speed Racer
Bike.description: Opening burst — extremely high DPS in first seconds, then sustained
Bike.note: Confirmed primary scoring engine in Nation Duel wave attrition

Storm Trooper.name: EC (Electromagnetic Charge)
Storm Trooper.description: Targets back row priority — stack or spread

EMP.name: Laser
EMP.description: Every 3 attacks triggers a devastating laser beam dealing damage to up to 3 units
EMP.note: Similar to Death Cannon Bomb Launcher — AoE back-row unit hitting multiple targets per proc

Rocket.name: Overshoot
Rocket.description: Inferior to Storm Trooper in all dimensions

comparison.verdict: More units per troop but weaker individually
High reduction but only 1 unit per troop — needs stat verification
Weakest passive — reflection adds minor offensive value

front_row_analysis.nation_duel_validated: Destruction Tank T13 — confirmed best in 5-run empirical test
